The agent identifies that none of the stated reasons are supported by the retrieved obstetric evidence, making evaluation impossible without relying on general dental knowledge. It concludes that “increased metal burnishability” is not a legitimate purpose of gingival margin preparation—unlike other factors such as removing weak enamel rods, achieving lap sliding fit, or ensuring self-cleaning surfaces—which are clinically appropriate goals.
